delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: This semester will focus on the study and analysis of a variety of World Englishes. As well a the study of World Englishes, the concept of English as a lingua franca will be introduced and the controversial English pronunciation model, the Lingua Franca Core will be debated.
aims: To provide students with a deep understanding of the concept of World Englishes, exploring the linguistic, cultural, and historical factors that contribute to the diverse forms of English spoken around the world. In the second half of the semester the aim is to equip students with the skills necessary for effective communication in English as a Lingua Franca, focusing on clear pronunciation, intelligibility, and intercultural communication competence in situations where English serves as a common means of communication among speakers of different native languages.
goals: To provide students with a deep understanding of the concept of World Englishes.
To explorine linguistic, cultural, and historical elements of language.
To explore factors that contribute to the diverse forms of English spoken around the world.

plan.text:
Guided Research on Language Issues (Pronunciation Issues in NS Contexts)
Variety in native speaker (NS) language
Accent, Dialect and Pronunciation in the UK
Accent, Dialect and Pronunciation in the US
Accent, Dialect and Pronunciation in the Australia and New Zealand

Guided Research on Language Issues (Pronunciation Issues in NS Contexts)
Introduction to African American Vernacular English (AAVE)/Ebonics
Lesson 4: What is AAVE? What does it sound like? What is its origin?
World Englishes: Creoles and pidgin English
World Englishes: Short history in Taiwan, Malaysia, South Korea and Japan
World Englishes: Tok Pisin short lecture
Understanding English as a Lingua Franca (ELF) Part 1
Understanding English as a Lingua Franca (ELF) Part 2

textbooks: All materials will be provided by the teacher.
prereq: Students should have a reasonable understanding of English to comprehend a seminar held in English.
prep: Students will be required to review content of seminars outside of class and to read in preparation for future seminars.
teachers: ◎ ショーン バーゴイン
